theme_and_archetype.description: Cyberpunk 2077 immersive terminal UI. Aggressive contrast, deep void black, neon yellow/red accents, technical HUD elements.

rules.title_glitch: Apply custom .glitch class to the main H1

spacing_and_layout.container: mobile-first, max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen relative overflow-hidden bg-[#050505]

title.classes: text-4xl font-bold text-center text-[#FCEE0A] glitch tracking-widest uppercase my-8
title.details: Must have data-text attribute matching inner text for CSS glitch to work

camera_button.classes: relative w-40 h-40 mx-auto rounded-full border-2 border-[#FF003C] bg-[#050505]/80 text-[#FF003C] hover:bg-[#FF003C]/20 hover:scale-105 transition-all flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-2 group cursor-pointer

action_button.classes: w-full py-4 bg-[#FCEE0A] text-black font-['Orbitron'] font-bold text-xl tracking-wider uppercase hover:bg-yellow-300 transition-colors border-none relative overflow-hidden active:scale-95
action_button.decorator: after:absolute after:bottom-0 after:right-0 after:w-4 after:h-4 after:bg-[#FF003C] after:block

response_card.classes: bg-[#0a0a0a]/90 backdrop-blur-md border border-[#FCEE0A]/30 p-6 relative mt-6
response_card.corners: before:absolute before:top-0 before:left-0 before:w-2 before:h-2 before:bg-[#FCEE0A] after:absolute after:bottom-0 after:right-0 after:w-2 after:h-2 after:bg-[#FCEE0A]

settings_icon.classes: absolute top-4 right-4 text-[#00F0FF] hover:text-[#FFFFFF] transition-colors hover:rotate-90 duration-300 z-50 p-2

loading_state.classes: font-['JetBrains_Mono'] text-[#FF003C] animate-pulse flex flex-col items-center gap-4 text-center p-8 border border-[#FF003C]/20 bg-[#FF003C]/5

visual_effects.css_glitch: .glitch { position: relative; color: #FCEE0A; text-shadow: 0 0 5px currentColor; animation: glitch 1s infinite; } .glitch::before, .glitch::after { content: attr(data-text); position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; overflow: hidden; } .glitch::before { left: 2px; text-shadow: -2px 0 #FF003C; animation: glitch-before 0.5s infinite linear alternate-reverse; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); } .glitch::after { left: -2px; text-shadow: -2px 0 #00F0FF; animation: glitch-after 0.5s infinite linear alternate-reverse; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); } @keyframes glitch { 0%, 100% { transform: skew(0deg); } 20% { transform: skew(-5deg); } 40% { transform: skew(5deg); } } @keyframes glitch-before { 0% { clip: rect(10px, 9999px, 50px, 0); } 100% { clip: rect(30px, 9999px, 80px, 0); } } @keyframes glitch-after { 0% { clip: rect(20px, 9999px, 60px, 0); } 100% { clip: rect(40px, 9999px, 90px, 0); } }
visual_effects.cyberpunk_grid_bg_css: .bg-cyber-grid { background-size: 40px 40px; background-image: linear-gradient(to right, rgba(255, 0, 60, 0.1) 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 0, 60, 0.1) 1px, transparent 1px); }

background_image.description: Deep void black base with subtle glowing neon yellow and red grid lines.

glitch_texture.description: Abstract dark glitch art texture for blending over backgrounds.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Fonts: In index.html, import 'Orbitron' and 'JetBrains Mono' from Google Fonts (e.g., <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;700&family=Orbitron:wght@400;700;900&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">).
2. Global CSS: Inject the exact 'css_glitch' and 'cyberpunk_grid_bg_css' snippets provided in 'visual_effects' into /app/frontend/src/index.css.

3. Tailwind Config: Do NOT rely on arbitrary values. Ensure colors (cyberpunk-yellow: #FCEE0A, neon-red: #FF003C, neon-cyan: #00F0FF) are correctly mapped if needed, or just use the exact hex codes directly in the classes.

4. Layout Structure: Use a mobile-first container wrapper `min-h-screen bg-[#050505] text-white relative overflow-hidden flex flex-col`. Apply the generated background_image URL via inline style or standard img tag with `opacity-20` and `object-cover` absolute behind all content, OR use the `.bg-cyber-grid` class if the image fails.

5. Header: The H1 MUST be 'NIGHT CITY ASSISTANT'. CRITICAL: You must add `className="glitch ..."` and the exact attribute `data-text="NIGHT CITY ASSISTANT"` to the H1 element for the CSS pseudo-element glitch effect to function. Use Orbitron font.

6. Camera Button: Build a massive, highly visible circular button. Use `<label>` wrapped around `<input type="file" accept="image/*" capture="environment" className="hidden" />`. Style the label with the 'camera_button' classes. Include a secondary ghost button beneath it for 'Upload Screenshot' (without capture flag).

7. Image Preview & Action: Once an image is loaded, show a square-cropped preview with hard borders (`border-2 border-[#00F0FF]`). Beneath it, render the massive solid 'Get Hint' action_button with the #FCEE0A background.
8. Loading State: Display 'ESTABLISHING SECURE CONNECTION...' or 'DECRYPTING...' using 'loading_state' classes when waiting for Claude API. Make it feel like an immersive terminal boot sequence.
9. Response Card: Display the hint inside a highly technical bounding box. Use the 'response_card' and 'corners' pseudo-classes to emulate a HUD frame. The text MUST be JetBrains Mono, color #00F0FF.

10. Settings Dialog: In the top right corner, use lucide-react Settings icon with 'settings_icon' classes. Clicking it should open a completely un-styled, purely HTML/Tailwind modal (or Shadcn stripped of default styles). The modal should have a void black background, neon red borders, and JetBrains mono text for configuring API keys or prompt settings.
11. Testing IDs: Assign data-testid to critical elements: 'camera-input', 'gallery-input', 'get-hint-btn', 'settings-btn', 'response-container', 'loading-indicator'.
